creator: trociny@FreeBSD.org
text:
Looking at the code now I am not sure that the error flag is set properly in
all cases. I don't recall my reasoning when I was implementing it but the last
condition (prp->min == 0 && prp->max == 0 && prp->count < 0) looks wrong for
me now. It means "set error if we failed to count processes due to a system
error and both min and max are zero".

Looking at the net-snmp code, it treats the condition "both min and max are
zero" as "no processes matching this name should be run". I think bsnmp-ucd
should follow this semantic.

Please see the attached patch. Does it make sense for you?

-- 
Mikolaj Golub
